L. 101–508, § 11801(c)(4)1988—Subsec. (b)(3). amended par. (3) generally, reenacting subpar. (A) without change, revising and restating provisions of subpars. (B) to (E), and adding subpar. (F).Pub. L. 100–6471986—, (d), designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), inserted heading, and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 99–514, § 902(a)Par. (2). , struck out last sentence which read as follows: “In applying the preceding sentence to a financial institution (other than a bank) which is a face-amount certificate company registered under the Investment Company Act of 1940 ( and following) and which is subject to the banking laws of the State in which such institution is incorporated, interest on face-amount certificates (as defined in section 2(a)(15) of such Act) issued by such institution, and interest on amounts received for the purchase of such certificates to be issued by such institution, shall not be considered as interest on indebtedness incurred or continued to purchase or carry obligations the interest on which is wholly exempt from the taxes imposed by this subtitle, to the extent that the average amount of such obligations held by such institution during the taxable year (as determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary) does not exceed 15 percent of the average of the total assets held by such institution during the taxable year (as so determined).”Pub. L. 99–514, title IX, § 904(c)(2)(B)100 Stat. 2385section 597 of this titlePub. L. 101–73, title XIV, § 1401(a)(3)(B)103 Stat. 549Oct. 22, 1986Oct. 21, 1986Aug. 9, 1989

children.heading: Expenses
children.label: 1 Expenses
children.text: Any amount otherwise allowable as a deduction which is allocable to one or more classes of income other than interest (whether or not any amount of income of that class or classes is received or accrued) wholly exempt from the taxes imposed by this subtitle, or any amount otherwise allowable under section 212 (relating to expenses for production of income) which is allocable to interest (whether or not any amount of such interest is received or accrued) wholly exempt from the taxes imposed by this subtitle.

children.heading: Interest
children.label: 2 Interest
children.text: Interest on indebtedness incurred or continued to purchase or carry obligations the interest on which is wholly exempt from the taxes imposed by this subtitle.

children.heading: Certain regulated investment companies
children.label: 3 Certain regulated investment companies
children.text: In the case of a regulated investment company which distributes during the taxable year an exempt-interest dividend (including exempt-interest dividends paid after the close of the taxable year as described in section 855), that portion of any amount otherwise allowable as a deduction which the amount of the income of such company wholly exempt from taxes under this subtitle bears to the total of such exempt income and its gross income (excluding from gross income, for this purpose, capital gain net income, as defined in section 1222(9)).

children.heading: Interest related to exempt-interest dividends
children.label: 4 Interest related to exempt-interest dividends
children.text: Interest on indebtedness incurred or continued to purchase or carry shares of stock of a regulated investment company which during the taxable year of the holder thereof distributes exempt-interest dividends.

children.heading: Special rules for application of paragraph (2) in the case of short sales
children.label: 5 Special rules for application of paragraph (2) in the case of short sales
i
children.text: by any person making a short sale in connection with personal property used in such short sale, or

children.label: ii
children.text: by any other person for the use of any collateral with respect to such short sale.

children.heading: Exception where no return on cash collateral
children.label: B Exception where no return on cash collateral
i
children.text: the taxpayer provides cash as collateral for any short sale, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the taxpayer receives no material earnings on such cash during the period of the sale,

children.heading: Section not to apply with respect to parsonage and military housing allowances
children.label: 6 Section not to apply with respect to parsonage and military housing allowances
A
children.text: a military housing allowance, or

children.label: B
children.text: a parsonage allowance excludable from gross income under section 107.

children.heading: Pro rata allocation of interest expense of financial institutions to tax-exempt interest
children.label: b Pro rata allocation of interest expense of financial institutions to tax-exempt interest

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of a financial institution, no deduction shall be allowed for that portion of the taxpayer’s interest expense which is allocable to tax-exempt interest.

children.label: A
children.text: the taxpayer’s average adjusted bases (within the meaning of section 1016) of tax-exempt obligations acquired after , bears toAugust 7, 1986

children.label: B
children.text: such average adjusted bases for all assets of the taxpayer.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any qualified tax-exempt obligation acquired after , shall be treated for purposes of paragraph (2) and section 291(e)(1)(B) as if it were acquired on .August 7, 1986August 7, 1986

children.label: I
children.text: which is issued after , by a qualified small issuer,August 7, 1986

children.label: II
children.text: which is not a private activity bond (as defined in section 141), and

children.label: III
children.text: which is designated by the issuer for purposes of this paragraph.

children.heading: Certain bonds not treated as private activity bonds
children.label: ii Certain bonds not treated as private activity bonds
I
children.text: any qualified 501(c)(3) bond (as defined in section 145), or

children.label: II
children.text: any obligation issued to refund (or which is part of a series of obligations issued to refund) an obligation issued before , which was not an industrial development bond (as defined in section 103(b)(2) as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of the Tax Reform Act of 1986) or a private loan bond (as defined in section 103()(2)(A), as so in effect, but without regard to any exemption from such definition other than section 103()(2)(A)).August 8, 1986oo

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (B), the term “qualified small issuer” means, with respect to obligations issued during any calendar year, any issuer if the reasonably anticipated amount of tax-exempt obligations (other than obligations described in clause (ii)) which will be issued by such issuer during such calendar year does not exceed $10,000,000.

children.heading: Obligations not taken into account in determining status as qualified small issuer
children.label: ii Obligations not taken into account in determining status as qualified small issuer
I
children.text: a private activity bond (other than a qualified 501(c)(3) bond, as defined in section 145),

children.label: II
children.text: an obligation to which section 141(a) does not apply by reason of section 1312, 1313, 1316(g), or 1317 of the Tax Reform Act of 1986 and which would (if issued on ) have been an industrial development bond (as defined in section 103(b)(2) as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of such Act) or a private loan bond (as defined in section 103()(2)(A), as so in effect, but without regard to any exception from such definition other than section 103()(2)(A)), orAugust 15, 1986oo

children.label: III
children.text: an obligation issued to refund (other than to advance refund within the meaning of section 149(d)(5)) any obligation to the extent the amount of the refunding obligation does not exceed the outstanding amount of the refunded obligation.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Allocation of amount of issue in certain cases
children.label: iii Allocation of amount of issue in certain cases
I
children.text: all governmental entities receiving benefits from such issue irrevocably agree (before the date of issuance of the issue) on an allocation of the amount of such issue for purposes of this subparagraph, and

children.label: II
children.text: such allocation bears a reasonable relationship to the respective benefits received by such entities,

children.heading: Limitation on amount of obligations which may be designated
children.label: D Limitation on amount of obligations which may be designated

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not more than $10,000,000 of obligations issued by an issuer during any calendar year may be designated by such issuer for purposes of this paragraph.

children.heading: Certain refundings of designated obligations deemed designated
children.label: ii Certain refundings of designated obligations deemed designated
I
children.text: the refunding obligation was not taken into account under subparagraph (C) by reason of clause (ii)(III) thereof,

children.label: II
children.text: the average maturity date of the refunding obligations issued as part of the issue of which such refunding obligation is a part is not later than the average maturity date of the obligations to be refunded by such issue, and

children.label: III
children.text: the refunding obligation has a maturity date which is not later than the date which is 30 years after the date the original qualified tax-exempt obligation was issued.

children.heading: Certain obligations may not be designated or deemed designated
children.label: iii Certain obligations may not be designated or deemed designated
I
children.text: any obligation issued as part of such issue is issued to refund another obligation, and

children.label: II
children.text: the aggregate face amount of such issue exceeds $10,000,000.

children.label: i
children.text: an issuer and all entities which issue obligations on behalf of such issuer shall be treated as 1 issuer,

children.label: ii
children.text: all obligations issued by a subordinate entity shall, for purposes of applying subparagraphs (C) and (D) to each other entity to which such entity is subordinate, be treated as issued by such other entity, and

children.label: iii
children.text: an entity formed (or, to the extent provided by the Secretary, availed of) to avoid the purposes of subparagraph (C) or (D) and all entities benefiting thereby shall be treated as 1 issuer.

children.label: i
children.text: the requirements of this paragraph are met with respect to such composite issue (determined by treating such composite issue as a single issue), and

children.label: ii
children.text: the requirements of this paragraph are met with respect to each separate lot of obligations which are part of the issue (determined by treating each such separate lot as a separate issue).

children.heading: Special rules for obligations issued during 2009 and 2010
children.label: G Special rules for obligations issued during 2009 and 2010

children.heading: Increase in limitation
children.label: i Increase in limitation
children.text: In the case of obligations issued during 2009 or 2010, subparagraphs (C)(i), (D)(i), and (D)(iii)(II) shall each be applied by substituting “$30,000,000” for “$10,000,000”.

children.heading: Qualified 501(c)(3) bonds treated as issued by exempt organization
children.label: ii Qualified 501(c)(3) bonds treated as issued by exempt organization
children.text: In the case of a qualified 501(c)(3) bond (as defined in section 145) issued during 2009 or 2010, this paragraph shall be applied by treating the 501(c)(3) organization for whose benefit such bond was issued as the issuer.

children.heading: Special rule for qualified financings
children.label: iii Special rule for qualified financings
I
children.text: subparagraph (F) shall not apply, and

children.label: II
children.text: any obligation issued as a part of such issue shall be treated as a qualified tax-exempt obligation if the requirements of this paragraph are met with respect to each qualified portion of the issue (determined by treating each qualified portion as a separate issue which is issued by the qualified borrower with respect to which such portion relates).

children.heading: Qualified financing issue
children.label: iv Qualified financing issue
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “qualified financing issue” means any composite, pooled, or other conduit financing issue the proceeds of which are used directly or indirectly to make or finance loans to 1 or more ultimate borrowers each of whom is a qualified borrower.

children.heading: Qualified portion
children.label: v Qualified portion
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “qualified portion” means that portion of the proceeds which are used with respect to each qualified borrower under the issue.

children.heading: Qualified borrower
children.label: vi Qualified borrower
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “qualified borrower” means a borrower which is a State or political subdivision thereof or an organization described in section 501(c)(3) and exempt from taxation under section 501(a).

children.heading: Interest expense
children.label: A Interest expense
children.text: The term “interest expense” means the aggregate amount allowable to the taxpayer as a deduction for interest for the taxable year (determined without regard to this subsection, section 264, and section 291). For purposes of the preceding sentence, the term “interest” includes amounts (whether or not designated as interest) paid in respect of deposits, investment certificates, or withdrawable or repurchasable shares.

children.heading: Tax-exempt obligation
children.label: B Tax-exempt obligation
children.text: The term “tax-exempt obligation” means any obligation the interest on which is wholly exempt from taxes imposed by this subtitle. Such term includes shares of stock of a regulated investment company which during the taxable year of the holder thereof distributes exempt-interest dividends.

children.label: A
children.text: accepts deposits from the public in the ordinary course of such person’s trade or business, and is subject to Federal or State supervision as a financial institution, or

children.label: B
children.text: is a corporation described in section 585(a)(2).

children.label: i
children.text: such disallowed interest shall not be taken into account for purposes of applying this subsection, and

children.label: ii
children.text: for purposes of applying paragraph (2), the adjusted basis of such tax-exempt obligation shall be reduced (but not below zero) by the amount of such indebtedness.

children.heading: Coordination with section 263A
children.label: B Coordination with section 263A
children.text: This section shall be applied before the application of section 263A (relating to capitalization of certain expenses where taxpayer produces property).

children.heading: De minimis exception for bonds issued during 2009 or 2010
children.label: 7 De minimis exception for bonds issued during 2009 or 2010

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In applying paragraph (2)(A), there shall not be taken into account tax-exempt obligations issued during 2009 or 2010.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The amount of tax-exempt obligations not taken into account by reason of subparagraph (A) shall not exceed 2 percent of the amount determined under paragraph (2)(B).

children.heading: Refundings
children.label: C Refundings
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, a refunding bond (whether a current or advance refunding) shall be treated as issued on the date of the issuance of the refunded bond (or in the case of a series of refundings, the original bond).
